Sherri Silverberg.review_text: Professor Silverberg is a standout in the Accounting department. She makes ACCT 200 feel engaging and manageable. She conveys material with absolute clarity, ensuring you aren't just memorizing formulas but actually understanding the 'why' behind the numbers.  Highly recommend if you want a solid foundation in accounting!

Sherri is one of the best, most caring professors at U of A. She was very knowledgeable about accounting and gives lots of resources to succeed in the class. She wants every student to do good in this class and as long as you put in the work you will do just fine.

She's super sweet! Fast talker during her lectures but gives so many resources to help study for exams, which the bigger sections don't get as much. She's very willing to help and does weekly zoom review sessions. Will reach out if you miss class to give you the slides if needed, but mandatory attendance.

Genuinely one of my favorite professors. She really cares about ensuring that you pass. She offers many resources, like extra Zoom practice sessions and office hours, which I recommend attending to do well in the class. The only thing is, if you take her small section, she does care about you being on time and not being late to her class.
Great professor. So sweet, and actually cares about your learning. The class is difficult, but she makes it very manageable.

Multiple choice tests only with plenty of trick questions. 80% of your grade is based off 3 tests, if that isn't biased grading I don't know what is. Taking this class over summer with this professor is a bigger mistake than taking her during a regular semester, you're literally just asking for trouble.

Sherri was an amazing professor for ACCT200. Any bad reviews are probably because students didn't take advantage of all the resources or help that the class provided. The lectures weren't the most interesting but that's not because of the professor, just the style of the class. Study outside of class and you will get a good grade.

She is a great teacher. Very helpful and understanding if you just ask her or explain your situation. The lectures go by quick which can make it hard to comprehend things, the class has about 150 students but there is a discussion once a week that has about 25 students. There are lots of resources if you use them but the class is hard!

I really liked Sherri. She was super nice & understanding. She cares about her students & if you need help, she is always willing to make time. I didn't love her lectures just because she moved so fast, but also that was because we had limited time, so I understand. Overall, super nice professor, just the lectures were hard because they were fast.

Her ratings are definitely based on the class and not her as a professor. The class is definitely challenging and a good amount of work but she really lets you know all the resources available for extra help and she's super nice when you ask questions. I think the rating is definitely a little unfair because she really does push you to succeed.

She is the absolute best. So sweet, willing to work with you, and will help you in any way she can to make sure you pass. She's the reason I will be majoring in acct. You NEED to study to understand the material for the test and practice the material. I failed my first test and got above 80s for the remaining 3.
Good teacher and will help you if you talk to her. Has plenty of availabilities for extra help. Teaches the material a little fast but other than that she's great.

Sherri is a wonderful and sweet professor! Yes, the course has a lot of exams and there is a lot of information on it but it is an accounting course! She gives out a lot of extra help through labs, the extra practices, and being super accessible outside of class. She's a funny professor and I suggest you strike up a conversation & get to know her!
Most of the bad reviews are from COVID, she is really nice in person.

Sherri was the nicest professor I've ever had at the UA! She was incredibly kind, helpful, and always accessible. Even now after the class ended, I still stay in touch. The class was done very well and I always felt supported. Four exams you'll need to prep for, but study guides and dedicated review days are sufficient and helpful. Take this class!

Professor Silverberg was an amazing teacher and really helped me succeed in this class. I struggled in the beginning of the year, but she was always quick to reach out to me and offer ways to help. I went from receiving a 68% on the first exam to an 100% on my final due to her above-and-beyond commitment to helping us understand the course content.

She is a wonderful lecturer. It was an even better experience having her in a small section. 

Make sure to review the slides and practice problems before exams. Do not blindly read textbook chapters. Honestly, it is best not to unless you're looking up a definition, but even then, just stick to her lecture slides and your practice problems.

sherri is an amazing professor, I took her class this fall and it was a very easy class. I would say to read the textbook chapters as well as the slides and take notes before class, she can go over the slides pretty quickly. sherri gave out tons of extra practice and did plenty of exam reviews on each chapter. she also has Sunday reviews on zoom.
Take this class with Sherri. You won't regret it!

I attended a small class she hosted last semester and had a good experience. Sherri brings extra online review every Sunday evening, and you'll have plenty of material to prepare for the exam, which I guarantee will help you do well. Shirley will give you four chances to drop the lowest-scoring test, so the final exam is optional.

Sherri is definitely the best teacher you can have for ACCT 200. She's super sweet and cares for all of her students! Take notes on the lecture slides before class because she can go through the slides a little fast. Due to the small class size, she gives individual feedback on chapter assignments which is nice

She is the absolute best. She is so sweet and kind and it is obvious that she really cares about her students. During class, she can go a little fast but she will make sure that you understand the material through practice. She is very accessible outside of class and is very attentive. She is an amazing professor and I would take this class again.
Yes, accounting 200 is a more difficult class but Sherri does everything she can to make it easier. She genuinely cares about her students so if you get the chance to take her you should!

Sherri is one of the kindest instructors I've ever had. Very supportive and encouraging as well as enthusiastic about the material which genuinely does help. As for the material, it will be difficult if you don't put the effort in to practice and understand it. Accounting can be like learning a language and new way of thinking.

Sherri is one of the nicest professors, she cares about your success in her class and is always willing to help. Her class isn't easy and people who don't pass blame her for it, but it's truly not her fault.

She is a very nice and understanding professor, if you come to her with a problem that you are struggling. The exam prep was very helpful in guiding my study habits. They do take occasional attendance, but you are allowed to miss three classes. The optional final exam allows you to replace a low score non-cumulative exam, which I thought was nice.
Super sweet and very passionate about accounting.

Sherri did a great job in teaching the class but accounting itself is just hard. On the other hand she does give out plenty of homework but has the accounting lab mandatory. The accounting lab helped out a lot and she is very understanding as a teacher.

Sherri is the absolute BEST! She knows your name, is super responsive and accessible outside of class, and goes out of her way to help you succeed. She provides so much extra practice, office hours, and extra credit opportunities and is very knowledgeable in her topic. The absolute sweetest professor I've had, definitely take her class!!

I do not understand why Sherri does not have a higher rating. She is such a sweetheart and responds to emails quickly. She has plenty of office hours where she will help you understand anything you are missing. The class is hard, but it is Accounting and is not supposed to be easy. Easily my favorite pre-business teacher.

The professor is very knowledgeable about the course content and is always willing to answer questions and present information in different ways if a student is struggling. The class is mainly in-class lectures with occasional in-class practice problems and weekly online extra practice problems.

She is a wonderful instructor. I took one of her smaller ACCT200 sections, and I was able to receive an A through completing the extra credit and understanding the practice problems she goes over. I recommend focusing on practice problems over reading that long textbook. Boo.

I had Sherri Fall 2022 in a small lecture class for ACCT 200. She is amazing!! She WANTS students to ask her questions, and she has a Sunday Zoom for her classes to go over lecture material. She's very understanding and cares about her students. She also gives out so many resources to help students. I'm taking ACCT 210 with her next semester!
She is a good professor. She is very passionate about accounting and that makes it fun to learn from here. I also felt like she was more than willing to give students all the help they needed.

The material in this class itself is really not hard, you just need to practice it thoroughly and do LOTS of reading. Sherri is a pretty good teacher, lecture isn't all that helpful if you don't read the book beforehand. Tests are a huge portion of your grade.

Accounting 200 is a hard class and Sherri is not forgiving. The lectures are boring & hard to listen to. Professor SIlverberg is robotic in her lectures and it is easy to let your eyes glaze over. The tests are hard and have a big impact on your grade. Be prepared to work you ass off and study a lot.

Bro she talks so fast and moves on from one thing straight into the next. Clearly knowledgable and nice professor, but literally can't keep up to take notes, it's almost comical at times. Can't take another course with her purely for this reason.

I learned a lot in this class but most importantly during COVID 19 Sherri was one of my more understanding professors and helped a lot during the transition process, and did things for the best of the students. The content can be really hard as time so I HIGHLY recommend taking a lab! The labs were extremely helpful and it was easy to ask questions

I don't know why everyone says ACCT 200 is so hard, this was one of the easiest classes I have taken at UofA. Her lectures are very boring, which makes it hard to follow along and stay awake. Even so, there's no need to worry; as long as you do the homework problems and a tiny bit of studying you should get an A no problem.

This has been one of the hardest classes I've taken so far at UofA. She teaches extremely fast and it can definitely be hard to keep up. It's good to print slides prior coming to class to fill in the blanks during the lecture. The points are very heavy towards your final grade and need to basically ace them to get an A or good grade.

One of the worst teachers out there in the U of A she makes the exams so hard she rushes through the lecture she dosen't care about students and she just wants to make your life tough. Take the class with Rob Stussie he is was better than her.
Ms. Silverberg is fantastic. She is super responsive to answering questions, and her enthusiasm for the material is contagious. WIsh I could take ACCT210 with her.

Sherri is really sweet and willing to help as much as she can! She does her best to make a really tough class as simple as possible! Go to her office hours, that's what makes the difference! Print out the slides and pay attention in class.

Rob Stussie.review_text: Super hard class, but required for Eller and RCSC. Stussie is loved by many, but I find him to be pretty arrogant. Lectures are very dry and he is quite monotone, so I had to teach myself most of the material. The class is curved at the end which is nice, but you'll still have to work VERY hard. Go to office hours, think tank, etc

I love Rob! He is an amazing professor, and I enjoyed taking his accounting course. He supports his students in various ways, ensuring they have the opportunities and help they need to pass the course. Make sure to pay attention to the material, go to office hours if you can, ask questions, and read the chapter before coming to class.

We had a passive-aggressive exchange via email about him taking points off when I turned in an assignment by typing in my answers in Word instead of writing it by hand and scanning it in - but he got a speeding ticket that morning and he had a change of heart and gave me the points back lol. He's prickly, but he's a good guy, I promise.

if you're taking acct, then stussie is your guy. I have struggled here and there throughout the semester but he is really good at teaching this subject and very helpful to where I understand it pretty well now. also would recommend joining the lab that's offered for this class and to just keep up with the material because its easy to fall behind

Overall, a nice guy, gives a good amount of extra credit, so make sure to do it. The tests are weighted pretty heavy and it will take a good amount of studying to do well, but it's definitely possible. Attendance was graded, but wasnt always taken in class. Go to the test review and ThinkTank for sure.
Rob was one of my favorite lecturers I have had at U of A so far.  His lectures are clear, direct, and informative.  Although ACCT 200 can be difficult, he made learning the subject very doable.

Rob was an amazing professor and his lectures were very well structured. I learned so much in this class, and whenever I needed extra help I went to his office hours. He was very helpful and really cares about his students.

I've never taken accounting before and as hard as it was for me to understand Stussie did a great job explaining things. He is very straightforward on what the concepts are, what is going to be on the exams, and what he expects out of his class. He really cares about his students and making sure they succeed in this course.
very good teacher and class but you need to attend lectures and get extra help to do good
hard class but stussie made it pretty simple and is just so awesome king love him take his class.

This class was pretty hard but if you go to lectures and take notes it isn't too bad. Its a big class but Stussie cares a lot and is helpful in office hours. Also PeerPro offers this class for tutoring, I did weekly sessions and got an A in the class.
Took the condensed online class ACCT200(7 weeks), class went by quick but professor had good recorded content and provided lots of opportunity for engagement with both him and his aids.
This class is awful and so is the teacher. Periodt.

Professor Stussie is amazing. Accounting is a hard class but he is the professor you want to get you through it. He offers a lot of resources to help you and goes through the content in an efficient and easily understandable way. If you take this class definitely sign up for the optional lab with Chandler Brown if you can.

If you take the time and master the study guide which is not that hard, you will do very well on the test. The study guide has everything. Rob is smart and if you are interested in accounting you will find him interesting. Not a class you should ever skip going to.

This class is very difficult and the material is hard but he teaches it better than all the other accounting teachers. If you show up to class, take good notes and study a little before the exams you will be fine.

Rob was a rude, uncaring, arrogant professor. I had 100% attendance and studied hard, but still found accounting to be challenging. He was rude and disrespectful. In office hrs, Rob was condescending and unhelpful towards me when I asked him a HW question. In class, he is also rude and treats me with extreme attitude and irritation.

Meh, cool enough class but the guy obviously doesn't care about his students. He really just cares about how much people like him on a surface level rather than people liking him for being a good professor.

The arrogance behind his monotone lectures drove me up a wall. This is an extremely difficult class for most and he only seems interested in helping when its convenient for him. I was willing to put in the work but just felt the effort was not matched on his end.

Rob Stussie is the most amazing accounting professor ever! He is great about giving extra credit to those that will work for it and encourages those struggling to seek outside help. I went into this class knowing it would be difficult and am so thankful I had him as my professor. I would definitely highly, highly recommend him!!

He is a great lecturer who does a lot to make a challenging topic easier to understand. He goes through many practice problems to show us the exact processes to answer questions. I would advise reading chapters ahead of time though so it all clicks in class. He is also pretty open about what kinds of questions we can expect to see on the exam.
Impossible to pass this class.
Its the arrogance and lack of actual teaching/effort for me!

This professor does not teach at all so prepare to teach yourself. The recorded lectures arent even his. We are placed into breakout rooms each class where no one knows whats happening. Its honestly not even worth going.

Overall a really good class and Rob is a great teacher. As long as you attend class and take notes in class you will be in a good spot for tests. This class is difficult but if you actually study and pay attention you will get a good grade.

Rob Stussie is one of the best professors Ive ever had. He is extremely knowledgeable about the subject and is super passionate as well. He is always available to help his students and is super understanding when you have stuff going on outside of school. The class is tough but if you put in the effort it wont be too bad.

Rob is an inspiring teacher who gets it! He is flawless in his lecture style and he never messes up. Rob knows exactly what he's talking about and can answer questions in a breeze. He is the best Accounting 200 Prof at UofA and is 100% worth taking.
Loved professor Stussie

Rob is great. ACCT 200 in itself is a rigorous class but Rob does an excellent job in explaining stuff. Please please please go to his class and listen to what he says rather than jotting stuff down. He gives hints if a particular topic will appear on the test. Lots of extra credit too. Take Josh Hunter's discussion session if available.

He is a fast paced lecturer but if you keep up and go to all the lectures you will learn everything you need to for the tests. Do as much of the extra credit as you can and then study a day or two before the tests and you should be okay!

ACCT200 with Prof. Stussie is challenging but rewarding. Attend class, study diligently, and utilize resources like unstuck study to succeed. The exams are tough but the professor cares about student learning.

Great professor! His lectures were very engaging and informative. Difficult, but not impossible to teach yourself most of the material. The discussions were very helpful in reinforcing the content. The homework and comprehensive homeworks were also pretty easy, but you don't get many attempts unfortunately. A lot of exams, but just study a bit.

This course was quite difficult, but Prof. Stussie managed to make much of the content easy to digest. I would reccomend paying attention in the lectures, going to the discussion, and taking notes. Stussie also offers plenty of study opportunities for exams.

This class is challenging, but the professor truly wants the best for all his students. If you focus during his lectures, you'll do really well. The class is test-heavy, but he prepares you well and clearly explains what to expect. He also offers a lot of extra credit opportunities, so make sure to take advantage of them they make a big difference.
Overall, this class was engaging and laid out clearly from the start. If you study, you will do fine.

I hated this class. I'm not good at numbers, and his teaching style did not help when it came to learning the basics of accounting. Like he said, accounting is like learning a new language, and him reading off the slides he posts on d2l does NOT help at all. Seek help from outside sources for this class; do not rely on him as a helpful source.
Great guy, easy A if you can test well.

Accounting is a hard class regardless of the professor, but Stussie is great. He's good at teaching and a really great guy. People giving negative reviews just didn't try hard because there's tons of extra credit and the final is optional. My advice is to make sure you get 100% on all the homework and do the extra credit, it's there for a reason.
Follow the syllabus and this class should be an easy A, but be ready to study and prepare for exams.

While the tests are in-person, making them a bit harder to do well on, this class is still pretty easy and Stussie does a great job keeping students in the loop with when work is due. He also gives ample opportunities for small, but meaningful extra credit and practice before the exams.

Accounting itself is a hard class. I didn't study at all and I was failing my first two exams. If you would like to get C+ you really have to study. After I start to pay attention everything Rob says my next exams were perfect score. He makes everything clear, he really wants his students to be successful. You need to pay attention to his lecture!

Rob was a great professor, taught the material well, and responded to everything quickly. The course itself is pretty irritating if you prefer to learn by reading rather than lecture. You basically have to abandon reading the textbook to keep up, and I wish there were chapter synopsis documents to read instead.

He's the best lecturer I've ever had, engaging & informative. He gives enough opportunities for grade improvement while still making the assignments matter as well. He's the reason I chose accounting as my major. Highly recommended.

The best ran course in my years at UoA. The professor and assistants are very interactive with the class and questions. If you are serious about learning Accounting, I would highly recommend his course. 

That all said, the pace can be extreme, exams are closed notes/book, and there is no way to pass if you don't study diligently.

Great accounting professor. Although a good professor will only take you so far in this class. My biggest advice to achieving an A is doing the optional lab, all of the homework and extra practice, and creating a clear study plan before each exam. Overall he is a create choice when it comes to choosing a professor for your first ACCT course.
you need to let students use a note card for exams, have a heart dude

Very organized professor, the class is difficult but he does a good job providing all information during his lectures although that is all class will be. As long as you attend class regularly and stay attentive in this large class you will pass.
He does a really great job teaching a difficult class!! Go to class, pay attention, and you'll be just fine. You can tell how smart he is and he really wants the best for his students.

Accounting can be a tough class however Stussie made it a smooth transition into the difficulties that may be faced in Eller. He emphasized important material in lectures, and he made it easy to understand the concepts and formulas. Overall, he was a great teacher for a challenging class.
Goes over the material very carefully and slowly so everyone can understand. He really tries to make sure everyone knows the material and tries to tell you exactly what to expect on the tests.
Very hard course, harder than it should be but great teacher

This was a very difficult course, but Professor Stussie made it as manageable as possible. He is very good at teaching the confusing parts of information and provides lots of extra practice opportunities for extra credit and for studying.

ACCT 200 can be a difficult class, but Professor Stussie explains concepts thoroughly and clearly. He is always open to questions, both during and before/after class. If you use the many practice opportunities, you'll do fine in the class. Take the optional lab, as it helps reinforce the material. Exams are not open note, but multiple choice.

Great professor. Accounting 200 is a tough class but he explains the concepts very well. Tests are relatively easy and he prepares you well for them. Lots of extra credit and practice opportunities as well.

Stussie can talk really fast and burn through subjects but overall is an enjoyable professor to listen to. The lectures are fast and dense so you better be focused during class because you will get left behind. Weekly homework and small quizzes and 3 exams with class averages around 70. Pay attention in class and you will be fine.
If you put in the work you can succeed, but you must pay attention and take good notes. The first units are most important and missing one class can be a major setback.
horrible

Amazing professor who does a great job of introducing you to the new language of accounting. Time consuming class but there are tons of resources presented to you that will make it very easy to get an A if you put in the time. He is super helpful and very kind if he sees you are putting in the effort. Definitely recommend Stussie

Take Stussie for ACCT200! This is a hard class, but Stussie did a great job making sure you have what you need to understand the material. Show up to class and pay attention. Make sure to do the assignments and take advantage of the extra credit. I didn't take the optional lab and was just fine, but it can't hurt. Definitely study for the exams!
Great professor, consistent work but he makes the topics easy.

Great class and prof. Lecture every day w/ attendance points. Pay attention, do lab, do HW, study, and you will be fine. Not difficult, just all new information that you have to understand. Have to buy clicker and textbook. Nice guy, good teacher, enjoyed the class.

Stussie is a tough professor. Everything is very test heavy in this class and lectures he moves extremely quick. You need to show up to every class to be successful and take very good notes. He is not approachable whatsoever. I'd recommend taking the lab, extra practice and extra points. If you're not an independent learner, this will be tough.

Stussie is definitely one of the better ACCT teachers at UA. His tests can be difficult cuz there's so many little details. But he gives good homework to help you try and grasp the concepts. Do the homework, do the study guides, look over the lectures, and you'll be fine. He does do random attendance quizzes so be sure to show up to class.

If you truly want or need to take this course, join his lab. Do not slack. If you are confused, ask questions. His Exams are difficult. There is four and he drops your lowest grade. From someone who tried to do the minimum, you have to put work in to understand anything in this course. If you do not have to take this course, don't.

This class was only fairly challenging but professor Stussie made it very difficult to endure. Every interaction with him is like pulling teeth and his jokes never hit. Probably because of his monotone voice. Theres so many things I could say about this professor but in summary he made me hate a topic I actually really liked
Accounting is hard but Rob does his best to post resources to help you be successful. The class is manageable and with a little bit of effort you can do well!

Terrible teacher. Barely helps with any outside resources. Expects you to be able to learn everything just with the work that is given. When I askeed him for extra work and help he was unable to help me.

Rob is such an amazing professor, while accounting definitely isn't for everyone, and I'm assuming that's the reason for the low ratings, Rob definitely makes it much more bearable. He is clear with what will be on the test, has thorough reviews before the exams which are super helpful. Overall definitely recommend taking accounting with him <3

Rob is a fantastic professor, and was great in ACCT200. The class can be challenging for many students, but Rob truly does want to see you succeed, and provides SO many options and opportunities to sharpen your skills in the class. Grading was fine, as there is plenty of content so a few bad grades won't hurt you that bad.

People rating low did not want to put the work into this class and are blaming the professor. It may take a moment but honestly when you get the hang of things the class is fine. Rob has been a great and kind professor. He prepares u very well; hw and study guides are just like the test. Honestly wish I could take my next acct class with him!! :)

Rob is awesome - he teaches very well (albeit "boring") and provides way more material than youll ever need to do well. You absolutely need to work at it, but he provides the resources. My biggest gripe with this class is the use of clickers and the mandatory printing of everything. Otherwise, I'm super happy with the class and Rob.

The content was difficult to learn in 7-weeks, but it is very possible to get an A+ if you dedicate a lot of time to studying. Tests are about 75% of your grade and can be difficult if you do not understand the content. The professor is uninvolved and lectures are extremely boring. He offers about 15 extra credit points if you do extra practice.

Stussie may not have been my favorite, but according to some friends who took this course with the other professor, he is preferable. Really goes through and tries to explain everything, two HW assignments a week plus a larger at exam time,which can be done pretty easily with textbook. Exams proctored but all MC and pretty fair given course level.
Stussie is probably the best accounting teacher at UofA. A lot of people give him bad ratings because this is a hard class. If you don't put the work in outside of class, you will not do well.

Accounting is definitely not an easy course in general, no matter what prof you have, but Stussie's such a great prof. He really makes the material clear, provides so many resources for students to succeed, and he really knows what he's doing. You definitely have to work in this course, but that's just accounting. Stussie's for sure the prof to get

Stussie is by far my favorite teacher I have had within Eller so far. His class slides contain many examples and make it much easier to understand the difficult concepts. He may come off as intense at times, but you can tell he really loves accounting and knows what he is talking about. I'd strongly recommend taking his class.

It's a hard class like any accounting class and I took this class online but Professor Stussie was great at reminding us of due dates, homework and office hours. He's very understanding and answers anything we are confused on. I really liked how this online class was structured. It is a lot of work and a lot of reading but you will learn a lot!
hard material, but if you work hard you can succeed in a difficult class

Anyone who says Rob was a bad teacher did not put any effort into this class.  Rob is very concise and detail oriented and cares a lot about his students.  With the new online format he offers curves and if youre looking for an easy accounting class you wont find one.  He is very good at teaching the subject, just participate and you will do good

This class gets a bad rep. It's very clearly laid out and there are plenty of extra resources. If you just watch the videos, ask for help, and study for the test you'll be fine. Rob is a really caring guy and will respond to emails super fast. This class does eat up a decent amount of time but it isn't hard work. Just put in the work.
Rob did a great job teaching the material. He strived to understand and meet student needs while maintaining course expectations. He has a very clear voice!
Seemed like a nice guy but this class was just ridiculous in an online format. The entire class is basically tests and the lectures are not helpful at all.
super difficult content, but stussie did his best to teach online, offer extra credit and even curve the exams. It is a difficult class and you have to put in way too much work to be successful

Accounting, in general, is a very hard class. This class will make you work for it and you may need to dedicate time outside of class. However, Rob is probably one of the best professors to have for this class. He explains the concepts pretty well and is always willing to help. I would highly recommend having him as your professor.

So far, I've gotten a 96% first test and an 88% second test. The zoom lab sections r kinda meh. Halfway through the course, I stopped showing up to them, as your random groups tend to be quiet and unhelpful. Found it easier to do the worksheets myself. If you fall behind within the first 3 chapters, I would drop. Catching up seems quite difficult

Rob is the definition of hands-off learning. He taught absolutely nothing the whole semester, and instead expected us to watch recorded lectures from a completely different teacher. I was forced to teach myself everything because he did nothing the whole semester, and his TAs are just as useless. He is arrogant and makes you feel SO stupid 24/7.

Stussie seems to be a nice guy, but I am not really impressed with his course. I don't know if it is because of the pandemic, but I feel like he puts no effort in teaching this class. He put up video lectures and expects you to teach the material to yourself.

To be fair, I am sure the pandemic has had a pretty significant impact on this course. That said, I don't feel like Rob has been helpful at all. Every class he just puts us into breakout rooms to work with each other on problems. There is no real teaching involved and I don't feel like I am learning anything.

Professor Stussie has one of the best structured online accounting courses you will ever take. The lessons are laid out simply, lecture slides are detailed and HW is challenging yet helpful. Tests are built to be difficult, so hard work is required. EC is offered in the form of extra practice! This class is rewarding and difficult, as it should be
Not friendly. Not helpful. Rude!
If you dont have to take this class dont. He tries to act like hes friendly and there to help but hes not. He acts like hes better than everyone in the room.

Rob is by far the best professor for ACCT 200. He is a great lecturer and the way he explains even the most difficult concepts is always very clear. The class is obviously difficult by nature, but he is always friendly and cares about his students

He is such a great professor. He makes everything so clear in his slides and he explains in such a nice way in the class. Go meet him if you have doubts he will help you. Attendance is very important. he is really nice and sweet.

Robert Stussie is a good and caring professor. He even says on the first day that he understands how difficult the class is and can be. However, he gives good feedback and even helps give enough advice for help. He is also very fun to talk to during office hours and really helps understand how difficult accounting can be unless you practice enough.
He does *try* to be friendly and helpful. However, once I made a mistake and he made a snarky, rude comment that makes you feel terrible. I'll never forget how awful he made me feel that day.

Rob makes accounting at 8 am much more bearable - the material is very dry and can be difficult to understand at times. There is a good amount of homework, however the tests will determine your grade. I recommend attending the optional lab and going to every class - lots of material covered every day and a ton of clicker questions

Although, the class is hard and maybe one of the harder subjects I have had so far, he at least understands and is really supportive. He is also really nice and very fun to meet with during office hours. He can give good feedback about why your answers were wrong on the tests and can also help you study for the tests. Along with giving suggestions.
Great professor 10/10 would take his class again.
This man is a legend

Stussie is one of the best professors I have had at the U of A. He gives great lectures and speaks of relatable topics to make things interesting. Material is pretty heavy but he tries his best to make it easy to understand. Definitely go to class or things may get difficult. Clicker questions happen a couple of times during the semester!

Stussie honestly does his best to make a very challenging class as approachable as possible. He really truly cares about his students and their grades and is always willing to help. Keep up with your work, show up to class, and honestly give this class everything you've got. Accounting is hard but Stussie is the best to take it with! Good Luck!!
Bad personality, rude in office hrs, taught myself the material.
Professor Stussie isnt a bad teacher and explains the content really well so its possible to understand. He doesnt seem to care about the class and acts like he is above everyone in the room.

Rob is probably the best professor I've had at the UofA. He really cares about his students ability to learn and the material he covers. Accounting 200 is not the easiest subject by far, but with Rob you stand the absolute best chance of scoring an A.

Who thinks "amazing lectures" in an accounting class? This dry material is brought to you by one of the best professors ever. Cannot believe how much knowledge I gained just by attending class. Stussie is clear and defined. He makes this class more interesting than I could have asked for! It wasn't easy - but class attendance makes the difference!

Rob is a great accounting teacher, but he is monotone, do not take this class early in the day or you'll tend to fall asleep. Accounting classes at UA is generally hard but he tries to make it as understandable as possible. Beware there are not many points in the class, tests mostly make that up so mess one up and you're screwed!

Stussie is an awesome professor, Accounting 200 isn't an easy class but he teaches it so well,  and you can tell he know what he is talking about! He moves pretty fast in class but if you have a question he will stop and break it down until you understand.  You have to go to class and pay attention to be successful but he is always there to help!

Stussie is a wonderful teacher. Accounting 200 is a very challenging class and he does all he can to help out the students. He is almost always available to reach and if you go into his office hours he really tries to work with you so you can do well in the class. Acct 200 is designed to be tough but Stussie actually cares for his students grades.

Accounting is a very heavy subject and is needed for several business degrees. Stussie knows his stuff but is very monotone when presenting material. Lectures are very dry but he is willing to answer questions and go further into detail with answers.

Rob Stussie is an amazing instructor. He presents the information very clearly and doesn't make it any more confusing than it has to be. Accounting at the UA is a hard course, but he makes it MUCH more manageable. He is obviously knowledgable and can answer any questions you have. Loved this instructor!

At the U of A, Accounting 200 is a mandatory class for students applying to Eller, and is a rather dry subject if you're not really into accounting. HOWEVER, if you had to take it, Stussie is the best Professor to take it with. He's helpful, caring, and overall a great teacher. BE SURE TO  KEEP UP with the material and PAY ATTENTION in class!!!

He is extremely stern and you'll see him smile probably 4 times the entire semester. The material is hard to comprehend because he addresses the topics as if people are already familiar with accounting. He speeds through things and doesn't break down the basics. The avg. test grade is 70% and each test is around 20% of our grade.
One of my favorite professors! A really well organized class and Rob inspired interest in accounting.

ACCT 200 is tough but Rob makes it so much better. Although the class is lecture heavy, there are optional labs that help you get a better understanding of the week's materials. Rob makes every class entertaining and gives you the resources to fully understand accounting.

Rob is a great teacher. He goes through the material to make sure everyone knows what's going on, and will stop if you have questions. Be sure to do the readings before class and stay on top of it, because the first few weeks of class build up foundation for later chapters.

Rob is my favorite professor so far at the U of A (i am a sophomore). Would not recommend taking him at 8am because he is very monotone, but knows a great deal about accounting and makes it very relatable to life. Tests are difficult but the class is passable.

I had to GRO ACCT 200, & the 2nd time I had Rob. He's so flexible & understanding! If you talk to him, he'll reopen wiley assignments for credible reasons or emergencies; he really just wants everyone to succeed because he knows the class is hard. He even lowered the TCE response % just so we could get the extra point. CHOOSE HIS CLASS IF YOU CAN!

Rob is a cool teacher. Thought I was gonna get an A in the class, but kind of screwed up on the final. Definitely would take again. Oh yeah, try to take the later time of this class, 8 A.M. is hell to go to.
Professor Stussie is very derogatory and talks down to students. He acts like he cares but he clearly doesn't. His class is also very dry which makes it hard to pay attention to.

This is definitely one of those classes where you understand it, or you don't. I didn't struggle in this course, but I know others who had a very hard time. Stussie is by far the best professor to have for accounting 200, he is so knowledgeable its insane & his lectures are VERY helpful. Join the optional lab, go to class, study & you'll be fine

This class is difficult as is, but Prof. Stussie made the material easier to understand and easier to follow. The class is a little difficult, but if you stay on top of your reading, and make sure you pay attention in class you should be good!
Gives great examples. Tells stories that are funny & engaging about real life accounting work.

Rob is an easy going guy and good lecturer, makes everything understandable. Accounting can be hard but with Rob its not so bad, just show up, do the hw and study hard. Exams aren't the easiest but he makes up for it with the occasional clicker extra credit. Class was very organized down to most minute details, which was appreciated. Best choice.
An awesome professor!

This is not a fun class to take, because it can be hard but Rob is the nicest prof. I went into tutoring regularly and he was so helpful and really wants his students to succeed. Make sure to pay attention in class or you will drown on the tests, but if you need help go to his office hours! He gives extra credit at the end of semester too :)

Karen Otto.review_text: Great Professor, as long as you do the homework and read the textbook the tests should be a breeze, spent 2 hours a week at most on the accounting homework and reading. Extra credit homework every unit up to 3 points which is nice, and there are 9 attendance checks and 2 of them are dropped. She is a clear expert in accounting and a great prof
rips zynachinos

If you have to take accounting 200 at UA take it with Otto!! She made accounting as easy as possible and truly cares about her students. I prepared myself to have a difficult time in this class but attending her lectures and office hours made this course so much easier. The tests are difficult, but she makes things as easy as possible.

Otto definitely makes accounting a much easier subject to understand. The class is difficult only because accounting is difficult. She tries to make it as simple as possible and it seems like shes always willing to help.

One of the best professors I had at the U of A. I loved her class and she didn't make accounting seem scary or impossible. Loved her encouragement and passion for accounting!!! Take her class if you can!!!!

Super chill professor, very helpful and nice. She's genuinely great, but the accounting class itself is really tough and you either get it or you don't (I've done better in physics and calc). 3 exams and the final counts for 1/3 of your grade. Attendance is taken, and you also get a lot homework.

Makes lectures very clear and repeats information multiple times if you don't understand it on the first go. Goes over by a couple minutes every lecture. Breaks down the info well and believes that everyone can get an A in the class. Definitely a pretty easy class with a discussion every week and office hours if you need them. Don't need textbook!
She is a really nice person and really cares. However, accounting is not enjoyable but that is not her fault. All I gotta say.

Karen Otto is one of the most charismatic professors I have ever had. She teaches accounting very well in her lectures. If taking ACCT 200 with her go to all the lectures and do the extra credit and an A is totally achievable.
Karen was a good professor. The class is hard but she tried her best to make it understandable to us. She gave lots of materal to prepare us for the exams

Accounting can definitely be hard in the beginning, but go to office hours or do the lab because Karen wants to help. Extra credit and homework will help you on the exams, but go to lecture if you want to do well. Everything was explained very well and enjoyed her class.

For this class, it was important not to fall behind. Karen Otto provided lots of resources to understand the material. Overall good teacher and you just have to put effort into the class to get a good grade.

Super manageable if you actually attend lecture. She goes through her slides really quickly but does a bunch of example problems that help with the homework. You get what you put into this class so do the extra credit assignments to study for the exams and sign up for the optional labs!

Otto is a pretty good professor she deserves to have higher rating in my opinion. Acct 200 is one of the harder pre-business classes which is probably the reason for the bad reviews. You have to actually study.

Karen Otto is a very good teacher. The material is difficult, but her lectures make difficult concepts a lot easier to understand. There are lots of opportunities to ask her questions, and lots of small homework assignments that are easy if you take notes and pay a little bit of attention during lectures. Just be sure to stay on top of deadlines.
Otto is good at what she does but her lectures are super fast. Make sure to do all of the assigned homework because I got a 70 and two 50s on the exams and still passed
Very simple as long as you show up. Pull your own weight in the class and you'll do fine. She is quick to respond if you ever have a question and really cares about your learning
Really good at teaching the material as long as you show up. You get what you put in to this class. It is a sep up in difficulty, but manageable if you want to learn the material.

Everyone giving this professor nice ratings must be TA's. Do yourself a favor, and avoid taking her class if you can. I honestly think she gets satisfaction of seeing students struggle. Attendance is not mandatory but if you skip you wont pass. She have long boring lectures and rushes through them, course material are not well organized. 3/10. 4 me

Prof. Otto is very knowledgeable in the subject matter. She offers extra credit and a lot of extra help, even on the weekends. ACCT 200 is a very challenging class with a lot of content. Prof Otto does ramble in class and does not make the class engaging at all. In addition, she comes off as rude and condescending during lecture and emails.
Good lecturer, but of course accounting will be kind of hard

Otto is a great professor overall - have nothing negative to say about her. Acct200 can be a tricky class but if you study, do the homework, and put in the effort then you will be successful. Would highly recommend Otto for Acct200.

If you put in effort in this class, you will be fine. Otto is an amazing professor and does her best in the limited class time to get us to understand the concepts. ACCT isn't easy, but if you put in the work outside of class, it will come to you. Do the lab and EVERY extra credit HW assignment and you will succeed. Literally finished with a 102%.

Otto is a good teacher, but you have to work hard to get a good grade. Her lectures are long and she doesn't stop once to take a breath but it's definitely possible to get an A. DO EVERY PIECE OF EXTRA CREDIT

I am sure those who complain don't pay attention in class, this a hard class by itself, she does a great job explaining if you fall behind it will be your fault, don't expect anybody to hold your hand, but if you pay attention try to read the textbook but more importantly do your Homework, you will be totally fine.

Do NOT take this class if you can not focus in big 300 person lecture halls. She just reads word for word off of the powerpoints and if she is running out of time she skips half the powerpoint and tells us to learn it ourselves. She goes over the slides way too fast and her class is made for her students to fail.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS! Her tests are extremely hard and she does not lecture well at all! You will FAIL if she is your teacher. Most of the class fails every exam! Go take it SOMEWHERE else!

She pretty much just reads lecture slides word for word. Very monotoned voice so it's difficult to follow. She will talk super fast and skip slides making it hard to keep up with notes. She's kind and it seems like she wants the students to succeed, however, the average grade on our tests is around a 76% and the exams are extremely difficult.

Prefer her over Stussie. A lot of memorizing and understanding how/why everything works but it gets confusing so flashcards and studying is vital.The 3 exams make up a majority of the grade, chegg will help you with everything,GO TO CLASS for clicker points,DO THE OPTIONAL LAB. Def go to lectures close to the end cuz she gives extra points

She is passionate about accounting, but she can't make you interested if you're not interested. The assignments are clearly laid out at the start of the semester. Warning she does make you print out certain assignments to hand write. A clicker subscription and online textbook subscription are required. Attendance is required.

Accounting is a class when the professor mentions to do something to do it. The readings in the textbook aren't mandatory but help out a lot. When she recommends to make notecards do it. This class will take extra time to really understand what is going on, but she is very helpful if you have questions, and there is extra credit given in homework.

Otto Is a good professor teaching a difficult course. Accounting 200 is difficult but she does her best to provide her students with the best materials possible to pass in this class. With that being said, your grade is a reflection of the effort you put in to all the assignments
Great professor, very good at teaching. Optional labs help a ton. Exam reviews are very good and detailed.

Probably the hardest professor Ive dealt with at University of Arizona. She makes you memorize everything in the class and doesnt give any equations or help on the exams. The final exam is worth 33% of your grade and they dont curve it. Would recommend taking this course at a community college or not with Karen memorize or fail the class Otto.

Pretty much what you'd expect for ACCT200. Karen did a solid job, mostly just taught from the prerecorded lectures. Class is pretty tough but that's typical. Overall pretty average experience, she didn't leave me blown away but I also don't have anything bad to say. If I had to take ACCT200 again I wouldn't mind taking it with her.

Amazing Professor, really cares about the students. Very Helpful. Difficult class to begin with but she makes it as easy to get the information to learn the material as possible. One of the best professors i have taken at the U of A. She really cares about her students

I love Otto!! Accounting is hard but she makes it manageable. She randomly selects 6 days where she takes attendance. Also, there are in-class clicker questions on specified days, so definitely show up on those days! There is a lot of homework but I feel it was all helpful when studying for tests. Definitely sign up for lab!! It helps so much.

She just goes over the powerpoint in lecture that you can find online with fill-in the blanks. Accounting is straightforward in this class, so it was super easy. Online homework once or twice a week, 2 attempts each. Exams were easy if you went over study guide. She has an accent that is super noticeable. Clicker questions once week. So easy.

This class covers a lot of information and reading the book is crucial. Professor  Otto does a great job going over information in class and answering and questions. She is super approachable and makes this class more interesting then then the average professor would. This class was required and I'm really glad I took it with Karen Otto

Karen Otto was an amazing accounting professor; her lectures were all very clear, and she was always accessible for questions. We always knew what was going to be on exams and how to prepare for them. Overall a very positive experience and I would highly recommend taking accounting with Karen Otto.

This professor was amazing. If you're nervous about the subject but willing to attend class, listen to the lectures, and complete the homework- this will be the easiest A. I can't say enough good things about this Professor Otto.

Accounting isn't an easy course at all but compared to the other accounting professors she seems to be the best and Stessi is good too. Don't take Blanchard. Work hard in the class and do the lab and go to campus tutoring for extra help because listening in the lecture can be very difficult because its way too long.
Dr. Otto was super fun and I hope I can be in another class that she teaches. Just go to class, do the lab, and do the homework and you'll be fine.

Professor is OK. The class is pretty information heavy on its own and going to class doesn't really help. You have to teach everything to yourself. If there wasn't random attendance points and clicker questions I wouldn't have even gone to class. Overall she is not particularly helpful but I think it has more to do with the class structure than her

This is a hard class. But all Acct 200's are hard. She slows stuff down a little in lecture. Her office hours are normally too crowded to be worth going to. Tests are hard but if you study more then just the night before you can pass. Take the optional lab it will help even if it seems tedious.

She doesn't necessarily make the content easy for her students. While she does take time to explain it! She does not connect the lectures to every day and color coats slides which makes everything even harder to understand. Overall she does give a lot of assistance but does not make it easy to pass. Her exams  very hard and effect your grade badly

all_reviews.review_text: Professor Silverberg is a standout in the Accounting department. She makes ACCT 200 feel engaging and manageable. She conveys material with absolute clarity, ensuring you aren't just memorizing formulas but actually understanding the 'why' behind the numbers.  Highly recommend if you want a solid foundation in accounting!

Sherri is one of the best, most caring professors at U of A. She was very knowledgeable about accounting and gives lots of resources to succeed in the class. She wants every student to do good in this class and as long as you put in the work you will do just fine.

She's super sweet! Fast talker during her lectures but gives so many resources to help study for exams, which the bigger sections don't get as much. She's very willing to help and does weekly zoom review sessions. Will reach out if you miss class to give you the slides if needed, but mandatory attendance.

Genuinely one of my favorite professors. She really cares about ensuring that you pass. She offers many resources, like extra Zoom practice sessions and office hours, which I recommend attending to do well in the class. The only thing is, if you take her small section, she does care about you being on time and not being late to her class.
Great professor. So sweet, and actually cares about your learning. The class is difficult, but she makes it very manageable.

Multiple choice tests only with plenty of trick questions. 80% of your grade is based off 3 tests, if that isn't biased grading I don't know what is. Taking this class over summer with this professor is a bigger mistake than taking her during a regular semester, you're literally just asking for trouble.

Sherri was an amazing professor for ACCT200. Any bad reviews are probably because students didn't take advantage of all the resources or help that the class provided. The lectures weren't the most interesting but that's not because of the professor, just the style of the class. Study outside of class and you will get a good grade.

She is a great teacher. Very helpful and understanding if you just ask her or explain your situation. The lectures go by quick which can make it hard to comprehend things, the class has about 150 students but there is a discussion once a week that has about 25 students. There are lots of resources if you use them but the class is hard!

I really liked Sherri. She was super nice & understanding. She cares about her students & if you need help, she is always willing to make time. I didn't love her lectures just because she moved so fast, but also that was because we had limited time, so I understand. Overall, super nice professor, just the lectures were hard because they were fast.

Her ratings are definitely based on the class and not her as a professor. The class is definitely challenging and a good amount of work but she really lets you know all the resources available for extra help and she's super nice when you ask questions. I think the rating is definitely a little unfair because she really does push you to succeed.

She is the absolute best. So sweet, willing to work with you, and will help you in any way she can to make sure you pass. She's the reason I will be majoring in acct. You NEED to study to understand the material for the test and practice the material. I failed my first test and got above 80s for the remaining 3.
Good teacher and will help you if you talk to her. Has plenty of availabilities for extra help. Teaches the material a little fast but other than that she's great.

Sherri is a wonderful and sweet professor! Yes, the course has a lot of exams and there is a lot of information on it but it is an accounting course! She gives out a lot of extra help through labs, the extra practices, and being super accessible outside of class. She's a funny professor and I suggest you strike up a conversation & get to know her!
Most of the bad reviews are from COVID, she is really nice in person.

Sherri was the nicest professor I've ever had at the UA! She was incredibly kind, helpful, and always accessible. Even now after the class ended, I still stay in touch. The class was done very well and I always felt supported. Four exams you'll need to prep for, but study guides and dedicated review days are sufficient and helpful. Take this class!

Professor Silverberg was an amazing teacher and really helped me succeed in this class. I struggled in the beginning of the year, but she was always quick to reach out to me and offer ways to help. I went from receiving a 68% on the first exam to an 100% on my final due to her above-and-beyond commitment to helping us understand the course content.

She is a wonderful lecturer. It was an even better experience having her in a small section. 

Make sure to review the slides and practice problems before exams. Do not blindly read textbook chapters. Honestly, it is best not to unless you're looking up a definition, but even then, just stick to her lecture slides and your practice problems.

sherri is an amazing professor, I took her class this fall and it was a very easy class. I would say to read the textbook chapters as well as the slides and take notes before class, she can go over the slides pretty quickly. sherri gave out tons of extra practice and did plenty of exam reviews on each chapter. she also has Sunday reviews on zoom.
Take this class with Sherri. You won't regret it!

I attended a small class she hosted last semester and had a good experience. Sherri brings extra online review every Sunday evening, and you'll have plenty of material to prepare for the exam, which I guarantee will help you do well. Shirley will give you four chances to drop the lowest-scoring test, so the final exam is optional.

Sherri is definitely the best teacher you can have for ACCT 200. She's super sweet and cares for all of her students! Take notes on the lecture slides before class because she can go through the slides a little fast. Due to the small class size, she gives individual feedback on chapter assignments which is nice

She is the absolute best. She is so sweet and kind and it is obvious that she really cares about her students. During class, she can go a little fast but she will make sure that you understand the material through practice. She is very accessible outside of class and is very attentive. She is an amazing professor and I would take this class again.
Yes, accounting 200 is a more difficult class but Sherri does everything she can to make it easier. She genuinely cares about her students so if you get the chance to take her you should!

Sherri is one of the kindest instructors I've ever had. Very supportive and encouraging as well as enthusiastic about the material which genuinely does help. As for the material, it will be difficult if you don't put the effort in to practice and understand it. Accounting can be like learning a language and new way of thinking.

Sherri is one of the nicest professors, she cares about your success in her class and is always willing to help. Her class isn't easy and people who don't pass blame her for it, but it's truly not her fault.

She is a very nice and understanding professor, if you come to her with a problem that you are struggling. The exam prep was very helpful in guiding my study habits. They do take occasional attendance, but you are allowed to miss three classes. The optional final exam allows you to replace a low score non-cumulative exam, which I thought was nice.
Super sweet and very passionate about accounting.

Sherri did a great job in teaching the class but accounting itself is just hard. On the other hand she does give out plenty of homework but has the accounting lab mandatory. The accounting lab helped out a lot and she is very understanding as a teacher.

Sherri is the absolute BEST! She knows your name, is super responsive and accessible outside of class, and goes out of her way to help you succeed. She provides so much extra practice, office hours, and extra credit opportunities and is very knowledgeable in her topic. The absolute sweetest professor I've had, definitely take her class!!

I do not understand why Sherri does not have a higher rating. She is such a sweetheart and responds to emails quickly. She has plenty of office hours where she will help you understand anything you are missing. The class is hard, but it is Accounting and is not supposed to be easy. Easily my favorite pre-business teacher.

The professor is very knowledgeable about the course content and is always willing to answer questions and present information in different ways if a student is struggling. The class is mainly in-class lectures with occasional in-class practice problems and weekly online extra practice problems.

She is a wonderful instructor. I took one of her smaller ACCT200 sections, and I was able to receive an A through completing the extra credit and understanding the practice problems she goes over. I recommend focusing on practice problems over reading that long textbook. Boo.

I had Sherri Fall 2022 in a small lecture class for ACCT 200. She is amazing!! She WANTS students to ask her questions, and she has a Sunday Zoom for her classes to go over lecture material. She's very understanding and cares about her students. She also gives out so many resources to help students. I'm taking ACCT 210 with her next semester!
She is a good professor. She is very passionate about accounting and that makes it fun to learn from here. I also felt like she was more than willing to give students all the help they needed.

The material in this class itself is really not hard, you just need to practice it thoroughly and do LOTS of reading. Sherri is a pretty good teacher, lecture isn't all that helpful if you don't read the book beforehand. Tests are a huge portion of your grade.

Accounting 200 is a hard class and Sherri is not forgiving. The lectures are boring & hard to listen to. Professor SIlverberg is robotic in her lectures and it is easy to let your eyes glaze over. The tests are hard and have a big impact on your grade. Be prepared to work you ass off and study a lot.

Bro she talks so fast and moves on from one thing straight into the next. Clearly knowledgable and nice professor, but literally can't keep up to take notes, it's almost comical at times. Can't take another course with her purely for this reason.

I learned a lot in this class but most importantly during COVID 19 Sherri was one of my more understanding professors and helped a lot during the transition process, and did things for the best of the students. The content can be really hard as time so I HIGHLY recommend taking a lab! The labs were extremely helpful and it was easy to ask questions

I don't know why everyone says ACCT 200 is so hard, this was one of the easiest classes I have taken at UofA. Her lectures are very boring, which makes it hard to follow along and stay awake. Even so, there's no need to worry; as long as you do the homework problems and a tiny bit of studying you should get an A no problem.

This has been one of the hardest classes I've taken so far at UofA. She teaches extremely fast and it can definitely be hard to keep up. It's good to print slides prior coming to class to fill in the blanks during the lecture. The points are very heavy towards your final grade and need to basically ace them to get an A or good grade.

One of the worst teachers out there in the U of A she makes the exams so hard she rushes through the lecture she dosen't care about students and she just wants to make your life tough. Take the class with Rob Stussie he is was better than her.
Ms. Silverberg is fantastic. She is super responsive to answering questions, and her enthusiasm for the material is contagious. WIsh I could take ACCT210 with her.

Sherri is really sweet and willing to help as much as she can! She does her best to make a really tough class as simple as possible! Go to her office hours, that's what makes the difference! Print out the slides and pay attention in class.

Super hard class, but required for Eller and RCSC. Stussie is loved by many, but I find him to be pretty arrogant. Lectures are very dry and he is quite monotone, so I had to teach myself most of the material. The class is curved at the end which is nice, but you'll still have to work VERY hard. Go to office hours, think tank, etc

I love Rob! He is an amazing professor, and I enjoyed taking his accounting course. He supports his students in various ways, ensuring they have the opportunities and help they need to pass the course. Make sure to pay attention to the material, go to office hours if you can, ask questions, and read the chapter before coming to class.

We had a passive-aggressive exchange via email about him taking points off when I turned in an assignment by typing in my answers in Word instead of writing it by hand and scanning it in - but he got a speeding ticket that morning and he had a change of heart and gave me the points back lol. He's prickly, but he's a good guy, I promise.

if you're taking acct, then stussie is your guy. I have struggled here and there throughout the semester but he is really good at teaching this subject and very helpful to where I understand it pretty well now. also would recommend joining the lab that's offered for this class and to just keep up with the material because its easy to fall behind

Overall, a nice guy, gives a good amount of extra credit, so make sure to do it. The tests are weighted pretty heavy and it will take a good amount of studying to do well, but it's definitely possible. Attendance was graded, but wasnt always taken in class. Go to the test review and ThinkTank for sure.
Rob was one of my favorite lecturers I have had at U of A so far.  His lectures are clear, direct, and informative.  Although ACCT 200 can be difficult, he made learning the subject very doable.

Rob was an amazing professor and his lectures were very well structured. I learned so much in this class, and whenever I needed extra help I went to his office hours. He was very helpful and really cares about his students.

I've never taken accounting before and as hard as it was for me to understand Stussie did a great job explaining things. He is very straightforward on what the concepts are, what is going to be on the exams, and what he expects out of his class. He really cares about his students and making sure they succeed in this course.
very good teacher and class but you need to attend lectures and get extra help to do good
hard class but stussie made it pretty simple and is just so awesome king love him take his class.

This class was pretty hard but if you go to lectures and take notes it isn't too bad. Its a big class but Stussie cares a lot and is helpful in office hours. Also PeerPro offers this class for tutoring, I did weekly sessions and got an A in the class.
Took the condensed online class ACCT200(7 weeks), class went by quick but professor had good recorded content and provided lots of opportunity for engagement with both him and his aids.
This class is awful and so is the teacher. Periodt.

Professor Stussie is amazing. Accounting is a hard class but he is the professor you want to get you through it. He offers a lot of resources to help you and goes through the content in an efficient and easily understandable way. If you take this class definitely sign up for the optional lab with Chandler Brown if you can.

If you take the time and master the study guide which is not that hard, you will do very well on the test. The study guide has everything. Rob is smart and if you are interested in accounting you will find him interesting. Not a class you should ever skip going to.

This class is very difficult and the material is hard but he teaches it better than all the other accounting teachers. If you show up to class, take good notes and study a little before the exams you will be fine.

Rob was a rude, uncaring, arrogant professor. I had 100% attendance and studied hard, but still found accounting to be challenging. He was rude and disrespectful. In office hrs, Rob was condescending and unhelpful towards me when I asked him a HW question. In class, he is also rude and treats me with extreme attitude and irritation.

Meh, cool enough class but the guy obviously doesn't care about his students. He really just cares about how much people like him on a surface level rather than people liking him for being a good professor.

The arrogance behind his monotone lectures drove me up a wall. This is an extremely difficult class for most and he only seems interested in helping when its convenient for him. I was willing to put in the work but just felt the effort was not matched on his end.

Rob Stussie is the most amazing accounting professor ever! He is great about giving extra credit to those that will work for it and encourages those struggling to seek outside help. I went into this class knowing it would be difficult and am so thankful I had him as my professor. I would definitely highly, highly recommend him!!

He is a great lecturer who does a lot to make a challenging topic easier to understand. He goes through many practice problems to show us the exact processes to answer questions. I would advise reading chapters ahead of time though so it all clicks in class. He is also pretty open about what kinds of questions we can expect to see on the exam.
Impossible to pass this class.
Its the arrogance and lack of actual teaching/effort for me!

This professor does not teach at all so prepare to teach yourself. The recorded lectures arent even his. We are placed into breakout rooms each class where no one knows whats happening. Its honestly not even worth going.

Overall a really good class and Rob is a great teacher. As long as you attend class and take notes in class you will be in a good spot for tests. This class is difficult but if you actually study and pay attention you will get a good grade.

Rob Stussie is one of the best professors Ive ever had. He is extremely knowledgeable about the subject and is super passionate as well. He is always available to help his students and is super understanding when you have stuff going on outside of school. The class is tough but if you put in the effort it wont be too bad.

Rob is an inspiring teacher who gets it! He is flawless in his lecture style and he never messes up. Rob knows exactly what he's talking about and can answer questions in a breeze. He is the best Accounting 200 Prof at UofA and is 100% worth taking.
Loved professor Stussie

Rob is great. ACCT 200 in itself is a rigorous class but Rob does an excellent job in explaining stuff. Please please please go to his class and listen to what he says rather than jotting stuff down. He gives hints if a particular topic will appear on the test. Lots of extra credit too. Take Josh Hunter's discussion session if available.

He is a fast paced lecturer but if you keep up and go to all the lectures you will learn everything you need to for the tests. Do as much of the extra credit as you can and then study a day or two before the tests and you should be okay!

ACCT200 with Prof. Stussie is challenging but rewarding. Attend class, study diligently, and utilize resources like unstuck study to succeed. The exams are tough but the professor cares about student learning.

Great professor! His lectures were very engaging and informative. Difficult, but not impossible to teach yourself most of the material. The discussions were very helpful in reinforcing the content. The homework and comprehensive homeworks were also pretty easy, but you don't get many attempts unfortunately. A lot of exams, but just study a bit.

This course was quite difficult, but Prof. Stussie managed to make much of the content easy to digest. I would reccomend paying attention in the lectures, going to the discussion, and taking notes. Stussie also offers plenty of study opportunities for exams.

This class is challenging, but the professor truly wants the best for all his students. If you focus during his lectures, you'll do really well. The class is test-heavy, but he prepares you well and clearly explains what to expect. He also offers a lot of extra credit opportunities, so make sure to take advantage of them they make a big difference.
Overall, this class was engaging and laid out clearly from the start. If you study, you will do fine.

I hated this class. I'm not good at numbers, and his teaching style did not help when it came to learning the basics of accounting. Like he said, accounting is like learning a new language, and him reading off the slides he posts on d2l does NOT help at all. Seek help from outside sources for this class; do not rely on him as a helpful source.
Great guy, easy A if you can test well.

Accounting is a hard class regardless of the professor, but Stussie is great. He's good at teaching and a really great guy. People giving negative reviews just didn't try hard because there's tons of extra credit and the final is optional. My advice is to make sure you get 100% on all the homework and do the extra credit, it's there for a reason.
Follow the syllabus and this class should be an easy A, but be ready to study and prepare for exams.

While the tests are in-person, making them a bit harder to do well on, this class is still pretty easy and Stussie does a great job keeping students in the loop with when work is due. He also gives ample opportunities for small, but meaningful extra credit and practice before the exams.

Accounting itself is a hard class. I didn't study at all and I was failing my first two exams. If you would like to get C+ you really have to study. After I start to pay attention everything Rob says my next exams were perfect score. He makes everything clear, he really wants his students to be successful. You need to pay attention to his lecture!

Rob was a great professor, taught the material well, and responded to everything quickly. The course itself is pretty irritating if you prefer to learn by reading rather than lecture. You basically have to abandon reading the textbook to keep up, and I wish there were chapter synopsis documents to read instead.

He's the best lecturer I've ever had, engaging & informative. He gives enough opportunities for grade improvement while still making the assignments matter as well. He's the reason I chose accounting as my major. Highly recommended.

The best ran course in my years at UoA. The professor and assistants are very interactive with the class and questions. If you are serious about learning Accounting, I would highly recommend his course. 

That all said, the pace can be extreme, exams are closed notes/book, and there is no way to pass if you don't study diligently.

Great accounting professor. Although a good professor will only take you so far in this class. My biggest advice to achieving an A is doing the optional lab, all of the homework and extra practice, and creating a clear study plan before each exam. Overall he is a create choice when it comes to choosing a professor for your first ACCT course.
you need to let students use a note card for exams, have a heart dude

Very organized professor, the class is difficult but he does a good job providing all information during his lectures although that is all class will be. As long as you attend class regularly and stay attentive in this large class you will pass.
He does a really great job teaching a difficult class!! Go to class, pay attention, and you'll be just fine. You can tell how smart he is and he really wants the best for his students.

Accounting can be a tough class however Stussie made it a smooth transition into the difficulties that may be faced in Eller. He emphasized important material in lectures, and he made it easy to understand the concepts and formulas. Overall, he was a great teacher for a challenging class.
